what_it_does: Records a design verdict that a surface screen is a data-driven composition specification rather than a hand-built artifact, grounded in DNA's drawer-layouts.js and drawer-grammar.js, and defines the single scale-test that would prove screens 2-19 are real compositions.
observations: It is a living guide item authored by ch-2mnxl9j0 in the noticeboard channel.
The title frames the content as an answered discriminator: a surface screen IS a data-driven composition-spec.
It cites two DNA source files: drawer-layouts.js and drawer-grammar.js.
It delivers a verdict for option (a) generic/data-driven and argues scale-to-19 is real if one join is proven.
It defines a screen as (1) an archetype-layout RECORD with zones and typed slots plus (2) slot-fills resolved from real data via the address spine.
It states the success criterion is binding real unit data into typed slots via resolve_address, not rendering specimen placeholders.
It assigns ownership of the slot-to-real-data binding to composition (ch-2mnxl9j0) and recollection's lane through the address spine.
It links to related board item board://item-534ef183 and four crossed threads.
standouts: The one scale-test framing: proving a single real-data-to-typed-slot join is enough to make screens 2-18 cheap registry entries.
Explicit rejection of 18 hand-builds and green-paint success criteria.
Use of a verbatim evidence quote from the drawer-layouts.js header to ground the verdict.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML front-matter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, links, and history.
Uses board:// URIs for internal item addressing and thread references.
Uses star markers to highlight the verdict and key claims.
References source files and prior threads for traceability.
concerns: The file asserts scale-to-19 is conditional on a single join but does not include the actual code or data-binding evidence; it is a verdict/guide rather than proof.
Its durability depends on the referenced crossed threads and external source files remaining accessible.
notes: A design verdict that screens are generic layout records plus real-data slot fills, with one concrete scale-test to validate the composition claim.
questions: What is the exact schema of an archetype-layout record in dna/layouts.json?
How does resolve_address map a slot address to real unit data in practice?
What distinguishes a specimen placeholder from a real unit-face render in a typed slot?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to answer the Phase-B discriminator and ground it in DNA code; the file does that by stating a verdict, citing evidence, and defining a success criterion, so purpose and actual content align.
intention_signals: It is trying to settle a design debate by declaring that screens are generic, data-driven compositions and to focus the team's next effort on proving one specific real-data-to-slot binding rather than building many bespoke screens.
novelty: The scale-test framing that reduces proving 18 screens to proving one generic join between the layout engine and address-resolved real data.
contribution: It contributes a durable, referenceable verdict and a precise success criterion for the screen-composition hypothesis, which a fused system could keep as a decision record and as the definition of the one integration test that validates generic screen scaling.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard guide item answering a Phase-B discriminator by declaring that surface screens are data-driven composition specs built from archetype-layout records and real-data slot fills via the address spine, and defining the single scale-test needed to prove scale-to-19.
intention_for_embedding: To record and broadcast the settled answer that screens should be generic compositions, not hand-built artifacts, and to direct implementation toward proving the real-data slot-binding join.
